briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their purported AI-powered shopping app was discovered to actually be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware firm Synapse, highlighting systemic risks in BaaS (Banking-as-a-Service) models.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing interest in leveraging open banking data for financial services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis exploring how open banking frameworks are reshaping payments competition and the balance of power between banks, fintechs, and big tech.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving increasingly niche and unconventional credit card products, challenging traditional card issuer models.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[RBC acquires Pinch Financial to streamline its mortgage process](https://betakit.com/rbc-acquires-pinch-financial-to-streamline-its-mortgage-process/)** — *BetaKit* — Canada's largest bank has acquired Toronto-based fintech Pinch Financial as part of its push to digitize and simplify the mortgage application experience for customers.

- **[Canada's economy lost 84,000 jobs in February, unemployment rate ticked up to 6.7%](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/canada-labour-force-survey-february-2026-9.7127105?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada shed 84,000 jobs in February and unemployment climbed to 6.7%, raising concerns about the economic environment in which fintechs and startups must operate.

- **[Trump administration expands trade investigations to Canada, dozens of other countries](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/trump-trade-investigation-canada-9.7127596?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — The Trump administration has launched new trade probes targeting Canada and other nations over forced labour concerns, adding to economic uncertainty for Canadian businesses and financial markets.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's federal government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and committed to an open banking framework, a landmark moment in Canada's ongoing consumer-directed finance journey.

- **[Canada could still win the AI race](https://betakit.com/canada-could-still-win-the-ai-race/)** — *BetaKit* — Despite competitive pressures from the US and China, Canadian tech commentators argue the country retains key advantages in talent and research to lead in AI — with direct implications for fintech innovation.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Trump Signs Orders Aimed at Mortgage Access, Housing Costs](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-pmn/trump-signs-orders-aimed-at-mortgage-access-housing-costs)** — *Financial Post* — Trump's executive orders targeting mortgage affordability echo growing North American demand for fintech-driven mortgage solutions — directly relevant given RBC's acquisition of Pinch Financial and Atlantic Canada's own housing affordability challenges.

- **[New US trade probe targets EU, Canada, UK over forced labour](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cgrzvdqze5xo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — New US trade investigations targeting Canada could create headwinds for Atlantic Canadian exporters and increase demand for flexible, fintech-enabled financial tools to help businesses manage cross-border financial risk.

- **[Wyvern launches international partnerships in Agtech, oil and gas](https://betakit.com/wyvern-launches-international-partnerships-in-agtech-oil-and-gas/)** — *BetaKit* — Atlantic Canada-connected space data startup Wyvern is expanding its international footprint in agtech and energy sectors, a reminder of the region's growing capacity to produce globally competitive tech ventures ripe for fintech partnership.
